title: Multi-task next-stop horizon buckets instead of pure binary only
summary: Recent F1 work uses a classification head plus an auxiliary regression head for laps-to-stop, suggesting shared structure between imminent-stop classification and broader stop timing.
rationale: Recent F1 work uses a classification head plus an auxiliary regression head for laps-to-stop, suggesting shared structure between imminent-stop classification and broader stop timing. Prior research in this run already considered event-time framing; the new testable direction is a lightweight auxiliary sidecar rather than a full survival rewrite.
implementation_hint: For each training row, derive censored-safe auxiliary labels such as next-stop bucket {1, 2-3, 4-6, 7+ laps} where available; keep final censored rows in the binary task only. Train either a shared-network/tabular multi-task model or separate auxiliary model and feed OOF horizon probabilities back into the main GBDT as features.
expected_effect: Can reduce false positives far from the real stop by teaching the model shape of the whole approach-to-stop curve, not only the final lap.
risk: Auxiliary labels are censored near race end and after missing future stops; poor handling can inject bias and wash out gains.
